change_comments.message:
pretty good but soen renamign required and some small simplifications.
Also there seem to be quiete a few checks for empty collection that I don't think are needed (they woddl require additional tests scenarios to get 100% coverage too!)

Some NB comments I have given.

Also, your unit tests doesn't cover all branches added, that is why the patchset hasn't verified. Please see the issue below:

Rule violated for bundle cps-ncmp-service: instructions covered ratio is 0.97, but expected minimum is 0.98
coverage check not enough, we can debug on call together if you need help to see which ones are not covered :)
just need to restore some parameter check please

nothing objectionable as such.
also other team members have extensive reviews and comments already.

inline_comments.message: should be moved up to line #69
NB: move this line to just before you need it; just before line #119
I checked, in the method below you effectly do 2 iterations, nothing else. each iteration will do nothing as theer are no entries so this check is not needed
not needed below code will send empty array list too
same name as the plural but without s, see previous comment
is this really possible for data we have stored using our own code?!

is this really possible?
Dont code for scenarios that should not happen
Also would the code following below actual have a problem even if this was the case?!
dont get the status until you need it ie just before line #160
why a wrapper (with a misleading name) around an existing well named method?!
use a more positive example for javaDoc eg "ACCEPTED"
i would just call it dataNodeSelectors here

i thought we're not gonna delete the sub id until it is accepted 

i.e. not leaving the sub id list empty in the database

this method has duplicated lines of code with sending to dmi for create event ,i suggest refactoring those relevant create methods to handle both create and delete

see line 86 sendCreateEventToDmis - you can modify this
it seems you did more than adding an enum, please try to summarize the basic ideas that you implemented in this commit
Please add some javadoc for this new interface... similar to aboves...
You can extract this into a constant with meaningfull name
this is now to be up for a re groom with the team, errror scenario says that ncmp should do a 'fire and forget' for delete which means if it is the last one , just delete the whole entry already.
again this is repetitive code from line 55 ...
might not need this, maybe call a re grooming with team for the updated requirement sign off agreed

i think just 
Update subscription status should be enough

dont do .*

ims urpised this was not flagged by sonar or checkstyle but maybe because its a groovy file

dont base the title on the method name strictly as it may change 

when: 'method to update status is called' should be fine
wont repeat but for this and other use- use full name 'data node selector'

Can these really be null?!
Adding this line means you need 4 test scenarios instead of 1!
why complicate the name, why not just "createSubscrption" ?
why complicate the name, why not just "deleteSubscrption" ?

Where will this exception be handled? Is it not better to just log a warning?
(like you do in teh consumer)
no need for wrapper method, can all cmSubscriptionHandler directly on line #61

I am not a fan of constant (names) that do NOT add any value, please just use string literals. 
I can deal with SonarQuber warning in the DB
this is method parameter no need to duplicate in the method name

below code is not proving this...
I see no reference to data node selectors, maybe you need to examine the details of one of the parameters?

to proof methods are NOT call it is always better to NOT check the parameter to prevent false positives:
0 * mockDmiInEventProducer.send(_*)

(unless the method IS called with different parameters but I dont think that is the case here)

I think the asserting of logging was needed when we were NOT doing anything else.
but NOW we are calling a subsequent method asserting lof logging is no longer needed
not need since you test a subsequent method call
Overall very good new tests, well done!
I think there is a containsAll() method

suggestion: instead of variabel you could use string literals that convey meaning
eg 'id-to-remove'  and 'id-remaining'
if you use string literals this would be clearer
use _* or *_ I always forget which it is
I asked Lee Anjella and she thinks too we don't need these. Can you chat with her

since you are checking these are use when mocking behavior do not use the word 'some'
also these are not just random attributes... but 'notificationTypes' and 'notificationFilter' as per the call on line #101
in Groovy you can just use [0] instead of get(0)

legacy: the persistence service returns...

'a subscription is created'
legacy: each datanode selector is added using the persistence service
an event is sent to each DMI involved
dont add significant stuff with updating or inserting a new description about what you added

Why did you change this, for positive calls we need to try to be strict. 
I suggested to use *_ but onle for when you are checking something is NOT called

this is the only case where you were meant to use *_
(because of the "0 *")
dont think this is need anymore now as you no longer are checking any logs
the point of the new values was that you do NOT need named variable anymore just use the new string literals
only one involved here. So say 'to the correct DMI'

when I meant 'stricter' I meant actual expected values, not just 4 _ :-)
